title: Floral Chewy Egyptian Prunes Tagine
description: A fragrant, slowcooked tagine featuring sweet prunes, aromatic spices, and a splash of rose water, simmered with tender chickpeas and sweet potatoes for a satisfying eggfree dinner.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a tagine or heavy‑bottomed pot over medium heat.
Add onion and garlic, sauté until softened, about 3 minutes.
Stir in cumin and cinnamon, cook until fragrant, 1 minute.
Add diced sweet potatoes, prunes, and chickpeas; coat with spices.
Pour in vegetable broth and a splash of rose water; bring to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let cook for 30–35 minutes, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are tender and sauce thickens.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Taste and adjust aromatics – a drizzle of lemon juice or extra rose water can brighten the dish.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs or toasted almonds if desired.
